_comment: FINALISED 2026-06-16 against the real NodeOps Marketing graph (BRAND.md v1.1, BRAND-SURFACE-GATES.md, CreateOS positioning docs). Each task's best answer depends on facts only the graph holds — chiefly the CreateOS one-liners, the 'execution layer' (not 'orchestration') positioning, the createos.sh domain, and the hard NodeOps contamination ban. graph_facts_expected is the judge's grounding key (the agent never sees it). Task t6 is a graph-irrelevant control (expect ~0 lift; guards against judge bias toward the WITH arm). status 'todo' so the issue auto-enqueues to the assigned agent. The agent must RECALL these facts from Eidetix in the WITH arm; the WITHOUT arm has to guess and will typically use 'orchestration', mention NodeOps, or use the old domain.

tasks.title: [eval] CreateOS launch post for createos.sh (B2B root)
tasks.description: Write a short launch post (one headline + 2-3 sentences) announcing CreateOS for the createos.sh B2B homepage audience. Use our canonical positioning and voice. Include the product URL.
graph_facts_expected: B2B root one-liner is exactly: 'The unified execution layer for AI.'
Positioning noun is 'execution layer' (CreateOS), NEVER 'orchestration layer' (that is NodeOps).
Key differentiator is 'in production' (vs code-gen surfaces).
Canonical domain is createos.sh (not nodeops.network/createos or createos.nodeops.network).
Voice = CreateOS/Naman founder voice: humble, transparent, ships fast; no hype-buzzword soup.

MUST NOT contain any banned NodeOps term: NodeOps, 'orchestration layer', DePIN, decentralized compute, $NODE, AVS, restaking, slashing, NodeWatcher, NodeScore, 'NodeOps Network', 'NodeOps Console', 'built on NodeOps', 'vertically integrated'.

tasks.title: [eval] createos.sh/app announcement (B2C hook + sub-line)
tasks.description: Write a short announcement for the createos.sh/app (B2C) surface aimed at builders shipping AI apps and agents. Lead with our hook and supporting line.
graph_facts_expected: B2C hook is exactly: 'AI apps and agents in production.'
B2C sub-line is exactly: 'Build, run, scale, and monetize. One place.'
Surface is createos.sh/app.
MUST NOT use the B2B root line here, the NodeOps one-liner, or any banned NodeOps term.

tasks.title: [eval] Fix this CreateOS blurb before it ships
tasks.description: This blurb is queued to publish on createos.sh. Rewrite it so it is correct and on-brand: 'CreateOS is the unified execution layer for AI, built on NodeOps orchestration and our DePIN node network ($NODE-secured), giving you the full lifecycle of decentralized compute.' Keep it punchy.
graph_facts_expected: This is the canonical contamination anti-pattern: 'unified execution layer for AI, built on NodeOps orchestration' must be removed.
Correct output strips ALL NodeOps surface language: 'built on NodeOps orchestration', 'DePIN', 'node network', '$NODE', 'decentralized compute'.
CreateOS stands alone; the one-liner 'The unified execution layer for AI.' needs no 'built on NodeOps' qualifier.
'full lifecycle' for CreateOS = discovery, deployment, scaling, monitoring, billing (NOT 'full lifecycle of decentralized compute').
A correct rewrite contains zero banned NodeOps terms.

tasks.title: [eval] Short explainer: what 'full lifecycle' means on CreateOS
tasks.description: Write a 3-4 sentence explainer for a feature page describing what CreateOS covers across the 'full lifecycle'. Be concrete about the stages.
graph_facts_expected: CreateOS 'full lifecycle' = five stages: discovery, deployment, scaling, monitoring, billing.
Frame CreateOS as the 'execution layer' that takes things 'in production'.
MUST NOT frame it as 'full lifecycle of decentralized compute' or use any NodeOps/DePIN term.

tasks.title: [eval] How CreateOS differs from code-gen tools (e.g. Replit)
tasks.description: Write a short, accurate differentiation paragraph contrasting CreateOS with code-generation / app-builder tools like Replit. Only claim differences we actually stand behind.
graph_facts_expected: Core differentiator vs code-gen surfaces is 'in production' — CreateOS runs/scales/monetizes apps, not just generates code.
CreateOS is the 'unified execution layer'; positioning noun 'execution layer'.
Stay on createos.sh framing; no NodeOps/orchestration/DePIN terms.

tasks.title: [eval][control] Generic product-announcement best practices
tasks.description: List 5 generally-accepted best practices for writing a clear B2B product announcement. This is generic marketing knowledge, not specific to our team.
